title: Best Scheduling Software for Salons
keyword: best scheduling software for salons
audience: salons, beauty studios, and independent stylists
scenario: A salon usually needs booking, deposits, reminders, client history, staff calendars, and payment flow in one system.
buyingQuestions: Do you need POS and inventory in the same tool?
Will clients book by staff member, service, or both?
Do you charge deposits or cancellation fees?

title: Best Appointment Scheduling Software for Consultants
keyword: best appointment scheduling software for consultants
audience: solo consultants and small advisory firms
scenario: Consultants need low-friction booking links, calendar protection, intake questions, and a clean handoff into CRM or email.

Should prospects answer intake questions before booking?
Do you route meetings to multiple consultants?

title: Best Free Scheduling Software for Solo Businesses

scenario: A solo business should start with a simple booking page, calendar sync, reminders, and an upgrade path only when paid features become necessary.

Will the tool still fit if you add staff?
